title: Build an AI Startup From Scratch

modules.title: Finding a Real Problem Worth Solving with AI
modules.sub: Spot genuine pain points that AI can meaningfully address
modules.description: Learners practice scanning their own experiences, communities, and industries to surface problems that are frustrating enough to pay to fix. They apply a simple filter to test whether AI actually adds value to the solution or just adds complexity.

modules.title: Validating Your Idea Before You Build Anything
modules.sub: Test your assumptions with real people before writing a line of code
modules.description: Learners design quick, low-cost experiments — landing pages, fake-door tests, and direct outreach — to find out whether anyone actually wants what they are imagining. By the end, they have real signal, not just opinions from friends, to decide whether to move forward.

modules.title: AI-Powered MVPs: What to Build and What to Borrow
modules.sub: Scope your first version so it ships fast and proves value
modules.description: Learners map out the smallest version of their product that would genuinely solve the core problem, then identify which AI capabilities to build versus which to borrow from existing models and platforms. They leave with a concrete MVP scope that a solo founder can realistically ship.

modules.title: Prompting and Wiring: APIs, No-Code Tools, and Glue Logic
modules.sub: Connect AI tools and build working product logic without deep coding
modules.description: Learners get hands-on with prompt engineering, API calls, and no-code platforms like Zapier, Make, or Bubble to wire together a functioning prototype. They troubleshoot real integration problems and build confidence assembling AI components even without a traditional engineering background.

modules.title: Talking to Users: Get Feedback That Actually Changes What You Build
modules.sub: Run user interviews that reveal what your product must become
modules.description: Learners practice structuring and conducting user interviews that uncover real behavior rather than polite opinions. They analyze patterns across conversations and translate those patterns into concrete product decisions for the next iteration.

modules.title: Pricing, Positioning, and Your First Paying Customer
modules.sub: Turn your working product into something people will actually pay for
modules.description: Learners explore pricing models common to AI products — subscriptions, usage-based, and one-time fees — and choose an approach that fits their audience and value proposition. They craft a positioning statement and practice the outreach and sales conversation needed to close their very first paying customer.

modules.title: Funding Paths for Young AI Founders: Grants, Accelerators, Bootstrapping
modules.sub: Understand your funding options and choose the right path for you
modules.description: Learners compare the real trade-offs between bootstrapping, applying to accelerators like Y Combinator or Antler, and pursuing non-dilutive grants aimed at young or underrepresented founders. They assess their own situation and build a short-list funding strategy aligned with where their startup actually is.

modules.title: Launch Week: Ship It, Measure It, Iterate
modules.sub: Take your product live and build a habit of rapid improvement
modules.description: Learners execute a structured launch sequence — choosing a launch channel, publishing, and driving their first wave of real users — then instrument basic analytics to track whether those users are getting value. They run their first post-launch iteration cycle, closing the loop between what they built and what the data and users are telling them.

synopsis: You don't need a co-founder, a CS degree, or venture capital to launch an AI product â you need a real problem and the right process. This course walks you through every stage from raw idea to live product with people actually using it.
rationale: Young adults are increasingly interested in entrepreneurship, but most startup courses aren't designed for the AI-native tools and zero-budget realities that define building in 2024 and beyond.
learning_outcome: After this course, you can take an AI startup idea through validation, build a working MVP using existing AI infrastructure, and acquire your first real users or customers.
source_signals: I am starting an AI+Education company
Ex-GitHub CEO launches a new developer platform for AI agents
